summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the marathon May 26 1973 Kezar show, highlighted by a smoking “They Love Each Other” and a soaring Truckin’→Other One→Eyes sequence.
review: The Miller‑patched SBD delivers an exceptionally clean, well‑balanced mix with superb stereo separation that lets every instrument breathe. Musically the three‑set marathon is a showcase of late‑spring ’73 energy – “They Love Each Other” smokes, the Truckin’ → Other One → Eyes of the World jam shines, and the second‑set China Cat > I Know You Rider feels tight and joyous. A few listeners note occasional buzzing and minor patch work, but overall the performance and recording are celebrated as among the year’s best.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, virtually all five‑star, praise for both musical performance and audio fidelity; only isolated complaints about buzzing keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with excellent stereo guitar panning; superb instrument separation. A handful of reviewers mention a low‑level buzzing/patching that is mostly inaudible on speakers but noticeable on headphones.

standout_songs: Truckin' > The Other One > Eyes of the World > China Doll
China Cat > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑hour marathon flows well; first set builds energy, second set delivers tight rockers and the famed China Cat > Rider, while the third set erupts into an extended Truckin’/Other One/Eyes jam that keeps momentum high to a rousing encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for front‑and‑center placement, soaring tone and inventive solos (especially on ‘They Love Each Other’, ‘Playin' in the Band’, and the Truckin’ sequence).
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for deep, resonant bass work; his solo during The Other One and the “ultrabass wizardry” on Eyes receive special acclaim.
band_member_comments.Bob: Guitar panning is noted as a strength; several reviewers call his rhythm work solid, though one mentions his sound as slightly too forward in one set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano/organ contributions are described as “having a good day,” with moments of Rhodes texture; a few feel he was occasionally a bit overpowering, but overall positive.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann is regarded as solid and driving throughout; no major criticisms appear.
